name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto||WD=|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}, {{ill|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami|1=Tatsuta Taisha}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948|1=Ryakunin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433|1=1238}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732|1=Eifuku-ji}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami|1=Tatsuta Taisha}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948|1=Ryakunin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433|1=1238}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732|1=Eifuku-ji}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * しながじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004741 == Commons category (P373) == * Shinaga-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡太子町山田3778', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shinaga-jinja.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} [[JA:科長神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11595950}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=May 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Shinaga Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Shinaga-jinja haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}} Yamada 3778 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 別名=Hassha Daimyōjin | 本殿=Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 例祭=4th Sunday of July | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shinaga Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Shinaga Shrine''' (しながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== Its principal deities are the wind deities {{ill|lt=Shinatsuhiko|Q1073925|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto and Shinatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Shinatsuhiko}}. The following are associated or merged deities: * Associated -- {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koya-ne|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * Merged -- {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|WD=Q246455|lt=Ame no Mihashira and Kuni no Mihashira Ōkami|1=Tatsuta Taisha}}, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Hondawake-no-Mikoto, Yasaka Ōkami, Dōso Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}, Amaterasu Ōmikami ===History=== Its founding origin is unknown. Originally it stood on {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} and was called '''Nijō Gongen'''. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Shinaga Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa District (Osaka)|Q11586399|WD=Q11586399|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa district}}, {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ryakunin|Q1193948|WD=Q1193948|1=Ryakunin}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1238|Q5433|WD=Q5433|1=1238}}), it was moved to its current location. There had originally been an Ebisu Shrine (also called Tsuchi-so Shrine) at the current site, and tradition says it became a {{ill|lt=massha|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906|1=Massha}} when Shinaga Shrine moved there. Until the Edo period it was called '''Hassha Daimyōjin'''. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' states: “When {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujiwara no Yoritaka|1=UNKNOWN}} moved Nijō Gongen to the current site, he invited and merged Kasuga deities, ancestral gods of the Fujiwara clan, and likely suppressed the old name Shinaga Shrine and called it Hassha Daimyōjin.” There is also a tradition that this was the birthplace of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, and among shrine treasures is a model helmet said to have been used by her. ''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'' notes that “Shinaga may have shifted from Shinaga -> Isanaga -> Okinaga, giving rise to the Empress Jingū birthplace legend from the Okinaga lineage.” It was ranked as gōsha on April 1, Meiji 5, and designated as a shinsen-heihaku offering shrine on January 28, Meiji 40. In Meiji 40, nearby Shinagaoka Shrine and Susanoo Shrine were {{ill|lt=shrine-merged|Q11590700|WD=Q11590700|lt=merged|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}}. ===Facilities=== Precinct shrines include Nijō Shrine, Ebisu Shrine, Kotohira Shrine, and Inari Shrine. This area was formerly the hereditary land of Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawamaro; at the shrine front is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}, and nearby are the mausolea of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|WD=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}} and {{ill|lt=Empress Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}}. Also in {{ill|lt=Eifuku-ji|Q11410732|WD=Q11410732|1=Eifuku-ji}} in Taishi is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinaga-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shōtoku Taishi mausoleum; Eifukuji-kita kofun). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Taishi (Osaka)|Q1347173|WD=Q1347173|lt=Taishi|1=Taishi, Osaka}} * {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}} {{commons|Category:Shinaga-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しなかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shinaga Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府太子町の建築物]] [[Category:小野妹子]][[Category:qq]]
